stimulus: Seawalls—tall barriers built parallel to the shore—are commonly installed to defend coastal property from storm surges. Seawalls can sometimes produce the opposite ______blank reflecting wave energy back onto the beach and accelerating the erosion of the very sand they were meant to keep in place.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Result" ends an independent clause, and a semicolon correctly separates it from the next independent clause, which begins with the conjunctive adverb "though," set off by a comma.

Choice A is incorrect because a semicolon after "though" would wrongly mark the start of yet another independent clause. Choice B is incorrect because using only commas creates a comma splice between the two independent clauses. Choice D is incorrect because, without a semicolon or period before "though," the two independent clauses are spliced.
